data.description:
## Problem
Findings carry a `severity` string (`severity_label(impact)` -> critical/major/minor/none,
`review_kernel/decide.py`) that is derived from IMPACT ALONE, so it does not order the same way as
the authoritative `priority = validity x impact`. A high-impact/low-validity finding shows as
`critical` while ranking below a `major`. This is a foot-gun: it invites reading the label as the
gate signal when it is not. It also caused a field misread — via `_KERNEL_TO_COMMON_SEVERITY`
(major->high, minor->medium, none->info) the review-bot surfaced the same finding as INFO then
MEDIUM across identical runs, reading like a severity change when only impact drifted.

## Idea
Make findings blocking / not-blocking + numeric priority, mirroring the intent that priority is
the single source of truth. Remove or rename the impact-only severity label so nothing mistakes it
for the decision.

## Why this is NOT a one-liner (traced)
- The label lives in the SHARED kernel (`pass3_decide`), so plan review carries it too — this is
  not a code-review-only change.
- The block/advisory decision is ALREADY binary and priority-keyed; severity is an extra surfaced
  signal, not the gate.
- Real consumers exist and there are TWO vocabularies (kernel critical/major/minor/none; and the
  original 5-level critical/high/medium/low/info in `findings.py`, from commit b501c38d1):
    - Gerrit review-bot comment surface (`review_bot/adapter.py`, ADR 0009)
    - cross-finding aggregation/dedup picks the highest-severity representative (`aggregate.py`)
    - workflow `fail_on_severity` policy (`workflow/steps.py`)

## Suggested sequence (each independently reviewable)
1. Rank aggregation by `priority` instead of severity.
2. Surface `priority` + blocking/advisory in the review-bot instead of the mapped label.
3. Migrate `fail_on_severity` to a priority threshold.
4. Drop the `severity` field from the finding shape.
Changes the Gerrit comment format and touches both gates -> warrants an ADR.

## Provenance
Discovered from the upstream review-code false-positive report (client project). Sibling of the
merge-base diff fix (ticket 8559-44c3-e08a-4bb9).
data.title: Retire the kernel severity label in favor of numeric priority (blocking/not-blocking)
